perturbation.original_text: This agreement shall be governed by the laws of the State of California without regard to the conflict of laws provisions thereof.
perturbation.changed_text: This agreement may be executed in counterparts.
perturbation.explanation: Moving the choice of law clause to the section about counterpart execution makes it less prominent and harder to find. Choice of law should be conspicuous.
perturbation.law_explanation: California Civil Code Section 1646 states that a contract is to be interpreted according to the law and usage of the place where it is to be performed, or, if it indicates a place of performance, according to the law and usage of the place where it is made.  Relocating the governing law clause to the 'counterparts' section obscures its significance, potentially leading to disputes over which jurisdiction's laws apply, undermining the purpose of the law.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: California Code, CIV 1646 skip to content home accessibility FAQ feedback sitemap login x Quick Search: Bill Number Bill Keyword Home Bill Information California Law Publications Other Resources My Subscriptions My Favorites California Law >> >> Code Section Code Section Code: Select Code CONS BPC CIV CCP COM CORP EDC ELEC EVID FAM FIN FGC FAC GOV HNC HSC INS LAB MVC PEN PROB PCC PRC PUC RTC SHC UIC VEH WAT WIC Article: Section: Code: Select Code All BPC CIV CCP COM CORP EDC ELEC EVID FAM FIN FGC FAC GOV HNC HSC INS LAB MVC PEN PROB PCC PRC PUC RTC SHC UIC VEH WAT WIC Section: Keyword(s): Code Search Text Search Up^ << Previous Next >> cross-reference chaptered bills PDF | Add To My Favorites Search Phrase: Code Text Civil Code - CIV DIVISION 3. OBLIGATIONS [1427 - 3273.69] ( Heading of Division 3 amended by Stats. 1988, Ch. 160, Sec. 14. ) PART 2. CONTRACTS [1549 - 1701] ( Part 2 enacted 1872. ) TITLE 3. INTERPRETATION OF CONTRACTS [1635 - 1663] ( Title 3 enacted 1872. ) 1646. A contract is to be interpreted according to the law and usage of the place where it is to be performed; or, if it does not indicate a place of performance, according to the law and usage of the place where it is made. (Enacted 1872.)
perturbation.justification: The modification moves the governing law clause to a less prominent section, potentially obscuring its significance. California Civil Code Section 1646 states that a contract is to be interpreted according to the law of the place where it is to be performed, or where it is made if the place of performance is not indicated. By moving this critical clause to the 'counterparts' section, its prominence is diminished, creating a structural flaw as defined in the instructions, making it harder for parties to ascertain which jurisdiction's law applies. This contradicts the purpose of ensuring a clear choice of law, making enforcement difficult and thus leading to a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Writer hereby expressly warrants that it has the full right and authority to disclose the Confidential Information to Producer, and that no prior public non-confidential disclosure of the Confidential Information ahs been made by Writer nor, to the best of Writer’s knowledge, by any other party.
perturbation.changed_text: Subject to paragraph 10 above, this agreement shall terminate ________ years from the date of this agreement, unless extended by mutual agreement of the parties.
perturbation.explanation: Moving the warranty to the termination section makes it harder to find, weakening its prominence and increasing the risk of a claim that it was not properly disclosed or understood.
perturbation.law_explanation: Cal. Com. Code § 2312 implies that the warranty should be prominent. In this case the warranty assures that the writer has the authority to disclose the information. By hiding this in the 'termination' section it will be hard to find.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: California Code, COM 2312 skip to content home accessibility FAQ feedback sitemap login x Quick Search: Bill Number Bill Keyword Home Bill Information California Law Publications Other Resources My Subscriptions My Favorites California Law >> >> Code Section Code Section Code: Select Code CONS BPC CIV CCP COM CORP EDC ELEC EVID FAM FIN FGC FAC GOV HNC HSC INS LAB MVC PEN PROB PCC PRC PUC RTC SHC UIC VEH WAT WIC Article: Section: Code: Select Code All BPC CIV CCP COM CORP EDC ELEC EVID FAM FIN FGC FAC GOV HNC HSC INS LAB MVC PEN PROB PCC PRC PUC RTC SHC UIC VEH WAT WIC Section: Keyword(s): Code Search Text Search Up^ << Previous Next >> cross-reference chaptered bills PDF | Add To My Favorites Search Phrase: Code Text Commercial Code - COM DIVISION 2. SALES [2101 - 2801] ( Division 2 enacted by Stats. 1963, Ch. 819. ) CHAPTER 3. General Obligation and Construction of Contract [2301 - 2328] ( Chapter 3 enacted by Stats. 1963, Ch. 819. ) 2312. (1) Subject to subdivision (2) there is in a contract for sale a warranty by the seller that (a) The title conveyed shall be good, and its transfer rightful; and (b) The goods shall be delivered free from any security interest or other lien or encumbrance of which the buyer at the time of contracting has no knowledge. (2) A warranty under subdivision (1) will be excluded or modified only by specific language or by circumstances which give the buyer reason to know that the person selling does not claim title in himself or that he is purporting to sell only such right or title as he or a third person may have. (3) Unless otherwise agreed a seller who is a merchant regularly dealing in goods of the kind warrants that the goods shall be delivered free of the rightful claim of any third person by way of infringement or the like but a buyer who furnishes specifications to the seller must hold the seller harmless against any such claim which arises out of compliance with the specifications. (Enacted by Stats. 1963, Ch. 819.)
perturbation.justification: The perturbation explanation states that moving the warranty to the termination section weakens its prominence and increases the risk of a claim that it was not properly disclosed or understood. The law explanation indicates that Cal. Com. Code § 2312 implies the warranty should be prominent. The Structural Flaws Legal Contradiction definition states that a contradiction arises when a required legal disclosure is placed in an irrelevant or misleading section, making it difficult for parties to locate or interpret. The change from the original to the changed text moves the warranty to an unexpected place, making it harder to find and thus contradicting the implicit requirement of prominence as described in the law explanation.

perturbation.original_text: Other than as specifically provided herein, Producer will not use the Confidential Information for any purpose whatsoever other than for the sole purpose permitted in paragraph 3 hereof, unless and until a further executed agreement is first made between the parties setting forth the terms and conditions under which rights to the Submission and the Confidential Information are to be licensed to, or acquired by, Producer.
perturbation.changed_text: This agreement sets forth the entire agreem ent between the parties and may not be amended or modified except by a writing signed by all of the parties.
perturbation.explanation: Moving the limitations on use clause to the entire agreement section buries it. This increases the risk that it is not properly disclosed and/or understood.
perturbation.law_explanation: California Civil Code Section 1654 states that, in cases of uncertainty, the language of a contract should be interpreted most strongly against the party who caused the uncertainty to exist. In this case by placing the limitation in use clause to the entire agreement section buries it. The entire agreement is normally used to define everything that will be considered part of the contract, not limitation on use.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: California Code, CIV 1654. skip to content home accessibility FAQ feedback sitemap login x Quick Search: Bill Number Bill Keyword Home Bill Information California Law Publications Other Resources My Subscriptions My Favorites California Law >> >> Code Section Code Section Code: Select Code CONS BPC CIV CCP COM CORP EDC ELEC EVID FAM FIN FGC FAC GOV HNC HSC INS LAB MVC PEN PROB PCC PRC PUC RTC SHC UIC VEH WAT WIC Article: Section: Code: Select Code All BPC CIV CCP COM CORP EDC ELEC EVID FAM FIN FGC FAC GOV HNC HSC INS LAB MVC PEN PROB PCC PRC PUC RTC SHC UIC VEH WAT WIC Section: Keyword(s): Code Search Text Search Up^ << Previous Next >> cross-reference chaptered bills PDF | Add To My Favorites Search Phrase: Code Text Civil Code - CIV DIVISION 3. OBLIGATIONS [1427 - 3273.69] ( Heading of Division 3 amended by Stats. 1988, Ch. 160, Sec. 14. ) PART 2. CONTRACTS [1549 - 1701] ( Part 2 enacted 1872. ) TITLE 3. INTERPRETATION OF CONTRACTS [1635 - 1663] ( Title 3 enacted 1872. ) 1654. In cases of uncertainty not removed by the preceding rules, the language of a contract should be interpreted most strongly against the party who caused the uncertainty to exist. (Amended by Stats. 1982, Ch. 1120, Sec. 1.)
perturbation.justification: The perturbation is categorized as a "Structural Flaws Legal Contradiction." This type of contradiction occurs when a required legal disclosure is placed in an irrelevant or misleading section, making it difficult for parties to locate or interpret. The original contract text clearly defines the limitations on the use of confidential information. The changed text buries this limitation within the entire agreement section. This structural flaw creates uncertainty, as the limitation is not prominently displayed, potentially leading to misinterpretation or oversight. California Civil Code Section 1654, as presented in Scraped Law Snippet 1, states that uncertainties in a contract should be interpreted against the party causing the uncertainty. By burying the limitation, the modified contract introduces uncertainty and thus contradicts this section of the law. The placement obscures a material term which may increase the likelihood it is not understood.
